records.ety: [OE. stonien, stownien; either fr. AS. stunian to resound (cf. D. stenen to groan, G. stöhnen, Icel. stynja, Gr. <?/, Skr. stan to thunder, and E. thunder), or from the same source as E. astonish. √168.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Stunned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Stunning.]

defs: To make senseless or dizzy by violence; to render senseless by a blow, as on the head.
quotes: One hung a poleax at his saddlebow, And one a heavy mace to stun the foe.

defs: To dull or deaden the sensibility of; to overcome; especially, to overpower one's sense of hearing.
quotes: And stunned him with the music of the spheres.

defs: To astonish; to overpower; to bewilder.
quotes: William was quite stunned at my discourse.
